2023-07-01.title: Why Bolsonaro Was Barred in Brazil but Trump Can Run in the U.S.
2023-07-01.abstract: In both the United States and Brazil, former presidents made baseless claims of fraud, and their supporters stormed government buildings.

2023-07-01.title: Wagner Uprising Highlights China’s Risks With Russia
2023-07-01.abstract: Xi Jinping needs Vladimir Putin to remain in power, and Russia to maintain stability, to help uphold the countries’ shared interests and to keep challenging the United States.

2023-07-01.title: Everyone Knew the Migrant Ship Was Doomed. No One Helped.
2023-07-01.abstract: Satellite imagery, sealed court documents and interviews with survivors suggest that hundreds of deaths were preventable.

2023-07-01.title: Mutiny Provided Glimpse of a Post-Putin Russia. Is the Window Still Open?
2023-07-01.abstract: Yevgeny Prigozhin’s revolt showed the possibility of a Russia after Vladimir Putin — and how pro-democracy forces may not be the ones that take over.

2023-07-01.title: Are Escalators and Moving Walkways Safe?
2023-07-01.abstract: A woman in Thailand lost part of her leg after it was caught in an airport walkway this week. The accident has gotten a lot of attention, but such injuries are extremely rare.

2023-07-02.title: Mayors Are Targeted in Fifth Night of Protests Across France
2023-07-02.abstract: Despite a generally calmer evening of demonstrations over the police killing of a 17-year-old, two attacks aimed at civic leaders highlighted the tinderbox situation.

2023-07-02.title: Japan’s Native Ainu Fight to Restore a Last Vestige of Their Identity
2023-07-02.abstract: A group representing the Indigenous people has sued to regain the right, lost over a century ago, to freely fish for salmon in a Hokkaido river.

2023-07-02.title: A Fatal Shooting and a Hijab Ban: Two Faces of France’s Racial Divisions
2023-07-02.abstract: The almost simultaneous police killing of Nahel Merzouk and a ban on head scarves in soccer were coincidental, but they illuminate France’s crisis of identity and inclusion.

2023-07-02.title: In Small Victory, Signs of Grueling Combat Ahead in Ukrainian Counteroffensive
2023-07-02.abstract: Expecting a quick retreat, a volunteer Ukrainian unit instead faced two days of tough resistance from dug-in Russian forces.

2023-07-02.title: Russian Forces Launch Overnight Drone Attacks on Kyiv
2023-07-02.abstract: The assault was the first pre-dawn drone barrage to target the Ukrainian capital in almost two weeks, officials said.

2023-07-03.title: Israel Launches Biggest Air Attack on West Bank in Nearly Two Decades
2023-07-03.abstract: Israel said it used drone-fired missiles and ground troops against militant targets in the Jenin refugee camp. The assault killed at least eight Palestinians, the Palestinian health ministry said.

2023-07-03.title: When Missiles Strike Kyiv, These Psychologists Race to Help
2023-07-03.abstract: Emergency responders in Ukraine’s capital offer not only physical care to the victims of airstrikes, but also mental health support for survivors in crisis.

2023-07-03.title: In Tuna-Obsessed Tunisia, a Favorite Food Becomes a Lot Less Affordable
2023-07-03.abstract: Tunisians put canned tuna on pizza, pastries and pretty much everything else. Don’t even ask for a tuna-free sandwich. But inflation risks turning an everyday essential into a luxury out of reach.

2023-07-03.title: Ukrainian Writer Victoria Amelina Dies After Kramatorsk Strike
2023-07-03.abstract: The 37-year-old poet and novelist became the 13th person to be killed in the missile strike on a restaurant in eastern Ukraine on June 27.

2023-07-03.title: Unrest in France Eases Nearly a Week After Fatal Police Shooting
2023-07-03.abstract: The number of arrests fell sharply following several nights of violence. But tensions remain high, especially after a string of attacks on elected officials.

2023-07-04.title: Zelensky Accuses Russia of Trying to Kill Georgia’s Former President Saakashvili
2023-07-04.abstract: Mikheil Saakashvili, now a Ukrainian citizen, has been imprisoned in Georgia. He appeared frail and skeletal in a court appearance that renewed concerns about his treatment in prison.

2023-07-04.title: Hong Kong Offers Bounties as It Pursues Dissidents Overseas
2023-07-04.abstract: The rewards of $128,000 for information leading to the prosecution of dissidents who have fled Hong Kong reflect stepped-up pressure to intimidate activists beyond the reach of the government.

2023-07-04.title: How ‘Barbie’ Landed in Hot Water in Vietnam
2023-07-04.abstract: Greta Gerwig’s upcoming film has been banned in the country for its use of a map depicting territory that both China and Vietnam claim as their own.

2023-07-04.title: Amsterdam Tries to Dim the Glare on Its Red-Light District
2023-07-04.abstract: The mayor wants to improve the neighborhood for residents, but sex workers oppose measures recently put in place. Now the city is looking to set up legal prostitution elsewhere.

2023-07-04.title: After Armed Rebellion, Putin Tries to Reinforce His Defenses
2023-07-04.abstract: Long focused on security, the Russian president is rewarding loyalty among the ruling elite and showering his most important constituency — the men with guns — with cash.

2023-07-05.title: Israel Says Deadly Raid on West Bank City Is Over
2023-07-05.abstract: A major military incursion into the city of Jenin left at least 12 Palestinians and one Israeli soldier dead. A Palestinian attack on Tel Aviv wounded at least eight.

2023-07-05.title: Rabbi’s Brush With Danger in Ukraine Went Viral
2023-07-05.abstract: A video put a fresh spotlight on the chief rabbi of Ukraine, whose renown predates his humanitarian efforts since Russia’s full-scale invasion.

2023-07-05.title: Biden and the Swedish prime minister meet to discuss Sweden’s NATO bid.
2023-07-05.abstract: President Biden and the prime minister of Sweden met at the White House on Wednesday, during a week of diplomacy by the two leaders to persuade opponents to allow the Nordic nation to join NATO.

2023-07-05.title: Kim Jong-un Wants a Satellite in Space. Seoul Says He Has a Long Way to Go.
2023-07-05.abstract: After analyzing debris from the North’s recently failed rocket launch, South Korea concluded that the isolated country’s satellite technology was useless.

2023-07-05.title: ‘Dig, Dig, Dig’: A Russian Soldier’s Story
2023-07-05.abstract: An inmate hoped to start over with a clean slate by fighting in Ukraine. Instead, he was confronted by the drudgery of trench work and the terror of battle. “You’re going in as meat,” he said he was told.

2023-07-06.title: At Least 16 Are Killed in Suspected Gas Leak in South Africa
2023-07-06.abstract: A cylinder with a type of gas often linked to illegal mining was found in a shack at an encampment near the city of Boksburg, an official said.

2023-07-06.title: Zelensky said he warned allies the counteroffensive would proceed slowly amid the wait for weapons.
2023-07-06.abstract: Ukraine’s president said he had wanted to start the military push sooner, in an interview with CNN.

2023-07-06.title: A Russian missile strike kills six people in Lviv.
2023-07-06.abstract: The authorities said more than 30 people had been injured and warned that there could still be people trapped under the rubble.

2023-07-06.title: NATO’s Welcome Party for Sweden Is Back on Ice
2023-07-06.abstract: Alliance leaders wanted their summit next week to celebrate the arrival of a new member, but stalling by Hungary and objections from Turkey have made that nearly impossible.

2023-07-06.title: Eton College Wrestles With Change in a More Modern Britain
2023-07-06.abstract: The youngest headmaster in the history of the English boarding school that has educated the British elite for centuries tries to navigate a tightrope between tradition and evolution.

2023-07-07.title: They Fell in Love in a Video Game. Now Both Are in Jail.
2023-07-07.abstract: The barrier-breaking romance of a Pakistani woman and an Indian man has led to criminal charges and nationalist intrigue.

2023-07-07.title: Zelensky will discuss the grain deal with Turkey’s president, with a NATO bid in mind, too.
2023-07-07.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine is wrapping up an intense week of diplomacy with a visit to Istanbul on Friday to meet with Turkey’s president about the Black Sea grain deal that is once again facing an uncertain future, according to Turkey’s state-run Anadolu news agency.

2023-07-07.title: Fire Breaks Out at Milan Retirement Home, Killing at Least 6
2023-07-07.abstract: The cause of the blaze, which also sent dozens of people to the hospital, was under investigation. “It could have been much worse,” said the city’s mayor.

2023-07-07.title: Donors Are War-Weary, So Ukrainian Soldiers Get Creative for Funds
2023-07-07.abstract: A robust fund-raising operation has been critical in supplying Ukraine’s military. But as the war drags on, contributions have lagged.

2023-07-07.title: U.S. Raises Pressure on China to Combat Global Fentanyl Crisis
2023-07-07.abstract: The Biden administration push comes after cooperation on halting the flow of the drug into the United States was derailed by wider geopolitical tensions.

2023-07-08.title: U.S. confirms talks with Russia on prisoner swap for Evan Gershkovich, but tempers expectations.
2023-07-08.abstract: The Biden administration and the family of the Wall Street Journal reporter take note of his reaching 100 days in jail.

2023-07-08.title: Bucha Gets a Remake, but Pain Lingers Behind the Facade
2023-07-08.abstract: More than a year after Ukrainian forces wrested back Bucha from Russian troops, the town is physically transformed. But so much remains unresolved.

2023-07-08.title: Toronto Prepares for Its ‘Beyoncé Weekend’
2023-07-08.abstract: Beyoncé is starting the North American leg of her Renaissance tour on Saturday in Toronto, where fans are giddy and the gridlock is imminent.

2023-07-08.title: Far-Right Parties Are Rising to Power Around Europe. Is Spain Next?
2023-07-08.abstract: As Spain prepares for elections, some liberal European politicians fear that the hard-right Vox party could become the first right-wing party since the Franco era to enter Spain’s national government.

2023-07-08.title: In Ukraine, They’re Doctors; in Britain, They’re Unemployed
2023-07-08.abstract: Often jobless or languishing in low-skilled posts, doctors who fled Russia’s invasion are forced to confront a difficult dilemma: see their skills go unused, or return to a country at war.

2023-07-09.title: NATO Tries to Remember What It Means to Fight a European War
2023-07-09.abstract: Supporting Ukraine and clarifying its relationship with the alliance will be a dominant theme at an upcoming summit. But NATO’s efforts to strengthen its own defenses along its eastern flank will also be key.

2023-07-09.title: This Paris Suburb Burned Before. Has Anything Changed?
2023-07-09.abstract: France spent billions of euros to revive poor areas after the protests that ignited in Clichy-sous-Bois in 2005. Amid another outpouring of rage, people want to know what went wrong.

2023-07-09.title: In Line? Online? Either Way, a Ticket to Frustration for Pantheon Tourists
2023-07-09.abstract: There is now a 5 euro fee to get into one of Italy’s most popular sites, but the process has been bewildering for some, and there are fears the system could be abused.

2023-07-09.title: For North Koreans in China, Seeking Freedom Is More Perilous Than Ever
2023-07-09.abstract: Recent efforts to help people escape Kim Jong-un’s regime via China have been thwarted by digital surveillance, profiteering and the pandemic.

2023-07-09.title: Number of Migrants at the Border Plunges as Mexico Helps U.S. to Stem Flow
2023-07-09.abstract: Mexico is taking steps to keep migrants from massing along the border. But as they flow into southern Mexico the calm may be short-lived.

2023-07-10.title: For a President and a King, the View From the Top Is Curiously Similar
2023-07-10.abstract: Despite hints of tension between Washington and London, President Biden and King Charles III share interests and have faced comparable challenges, which might have helped smooth their meeting.

2023-07-10.title: Leaders push to resolve disagreements on the eve of the NATO summit.
2023-07-10.abstract: President Biden and other NATO leaders are making a last minute push before an annual summit to address crucial disagreements that threaten to overshadow the alliance’s unity in opposition to Russia’s war in Ukraine.

2023-07-10.title: Six Dead After Stabbing at a Preschool in China
2023-07-10.abstract: The police in southern China arrested a 25-year-old man in relation to the attack. Knife attacks are not uncommon in China, and many have targeted schools.

2023-07-10.title: A Tangled Past Complicates Poland’s Welcome to Ukrainian Refugees
2023-07-10.abstract: Polish memories of ethnic pogroms by Ukrainian nationalists in the 1940s jostle with the trauma left by Russia and its war in Ukraine.

2023-07-10.title: Future of Wagner Leader’s Business Empire Is Cloudy After Rebellion
2023-07-10.abstract: Over decades, Yevgeny V. Prigozhin amassed considerable clout in Russia and built businesses in at least 15 countries. His whereabouts and the future of his extensive portfolio are now uncertain.

2023-07-11.title: North Korea Ramps Up Rhetoric Ahead of U.S. Nuclear Submarine Visit
2023-07-11.abstract: The country warned that it would shoot down American spy planes, escalating verbal attacks on the U.S. as South Korea’s leader traveled to attend a NATO summit.

2023-07-11.title: Flooding and Landslides Hit Japan
2023-07-11.abstract: The deluge hit the island of Kyushu with what one official called the “heaviest rain ever experienced” in the region.

2023-07-11.title: Sudanese City Becomes Center of ‘New Phase’ of War
2023-07-11.abstract: Fierce fighting has erupted in the city of Omdurman, across the Nile from the capital, Khartoum, in a desperate battle to control supply routes.

2023-07-11.title: Dirty Socks and Rotting Bodies: What Russians Left Behind in the Trenches
2023-07-11.abstract: As Ukrainian troops inch forward in the counteroffensive, they are occupying positions abandoned by enemy troops. Says one: “It’s not very pleasant.”

2023-07-11.title: Protesters Throng Israeli Airport After Government Moves to Rein In Judiciary
2023-07-11.abstract: Tens of thousands of demonstrators across the country caused widespread disruption after the governing coalition pushed forward with plans to limit the power of the Supreme Court.

2023-07-12.title: On the Front Line of a War Over Bullfighting Traditions
2023-07-12.abstract: Opponents of Spain’s comic shows at bullfights by people with dwarfism say they are banned by a new law. But performers say the show must go on.

2023-07-12.title: How a Peaceful Country Became a Gold Rush State for Drug Cartels
2023-07-12.abstract: In Ecuador, an intelligence official said: “People consume abroad, but they don’t understand the consequences that take place here.”

2023-07-12.title: Milan Kundera, Literary Star Who Skewered Communist Rule, Dies at 94
2023-07-12.abstract: The author of “The Unbearable Lightness of Being,” he was known for sexually charged novels that captured the suffocating absurdity of life in his native Czechoslovakia.

2023-07-12.title: Mozambican Accused in $2 Billion ‘Tuna’ Scandal Is Extradited to New York
2023-07-12.abstract: Manuel Chang, the former finance minister of Mozambique, is at the center of a scandal that roped in American investors.

2023-07-12.title: Biden Vowed to Bring Gershkovich Home, His Parents Say
2023-07-12.abstract: The parents of Evan Gershkovich, the American reporter jailed in Russia, said in a broadcast interview that President Biden had promised to do “whatever it takes” to secure his release.

2023-07-13.title: Insurgent Party Can Stay on Guatemala’s Ballot, Election Agency Says
2023-07-13.abstract: A prosecutor had tried to suspend the party, knocking the anticorruption presidential candidate Bernardo Arévalo out of next month’s runoff contest.

2023-07-13.title: Biden meets with Nordic leaders after progress on Sweden’s bid to join NATO.
2023-07-13.abstract: Fresh from a pivotal summit of NATO leaders in Lithuania, President Biden met in Finland, the alliance’s newest member, with Nordic leaders, who have drawn closer militarily to the United States since Russia invaded Ukraine last year.

2023-07-13.title: Junta’s Allies Reject Thai Election Results, Derailing Top Opposition Figure
2023-07-13.abstract: The Thai military’s hold on the Senate blocked a popular progressive candidate who had emerged as the clear winner. Parliament will now have to vote again, as the opposition vows to demonstrate.

2023-07-13.title: Bucha gets a remake, but pain lingers behind the facade.
2023-07-13.abstract: There is a line of tidy houses on Vokzalna Street, where crumbling homes once lined a roadway littered with burned-out Russian tanks. There are neat sidewalks and fresh pavement with blue and yellow bunting hanging overhead. And there are backhoes and bulldozers plowing across a construction site where a new home goods store will replace a previous one that was burned to the ground.

2023-07-13.title: Biden Pledges Long-Term Backing for Ukraine, but a U.S. Election Looms
2023-07-13.abstract: Citing American politics, some NATO leaders expressed concern that Washington could waver in its support if there were a shift in power.

2023-07-14.title: Syria Authorizes U.N. Use of Border Crossing for Aid, Giving Itself Oversight
2023-07-14.abstract: Under a previous arrangement, the U.N. and other aid agencies had Security Council approval to deliver humanitarian aid to Syria’s northern areas. That agreement, in place since 2014, fell apart two days ago.

2023-07-14.title: Cluster munitions reach Ukraine a week after Biden’s announcement.
2023-07-14.abstract: U.S.-provided cluster munitions have arrived in Ukraine, U.S. and Ukrainian officials have said, a week after President Biden announced he had made the “very difficult decision” to provide Kyiv with the widely banned weapon.

2023-07-14.title: Ruined, Empty, Mined and Overgrown: Ukraine’s Forgotten Villages
2023-07-14.abstract: Settlements along Ukraine’s eastern steppe were reduced to wreckage as the war passed through them. Now choked with weeds and with few residents, they are at risk of disappearing.

2023-07-14.title: Risk, Regulation and Personal Responsibility in Australia’s Last Frontier
2023-07-14.abstract: Setting off fireworks in the Northern Territory, with a sense of time running out.

2023-07-14.title: A Ukrainian commander describes the grueling battle against dug-in Russian forces.
2023-07-14.abstract: Troops with the 36th Marine Brigade have been the tip of the spear in Ukraine’s counteroffensive and have advanced about five miles into a bulge into Russian lines in southern Ukraine.

2023-07-15.title: South Korea’s Monsoon Rains Set Off Deadly Landslides and Flooding
2023-07-15.abstract: With at least 40 people dead, the current monsoon season is already proving to be a particularly dangerous one in South Korea, with rain forecast to continue.

2023-07-15.title: After Suffering Heavy Losses, Ukrainians Paused to Rethink Strategy
2023-07-15.abstract: Early in the counteroffensive, Ukraine lost as much as 20 percent of its weapons and armor. The rate dropped as the campaign slowed and commanders shifted tactics.

2023-07-15.title: Canadian Politicians Who Criticize China Become Its Targets
2023-07-15.abstract: As China increases its reach in diaspora communities, Chinese Canadian politicians in Vancouver are the focus of Chinese state interference in Canadian politics.

2023-07-15.title: Russian Forces Are Doing ‘Everything They Can’ to Stop Counteroffensive, Zelensky Says
2023-07-15.abstract: The Ukrainian leader has secured new weapons pledges, but it was not clear when the aid would arrive, or how consequential it would be on the battlefield.

2023-07-15.title: Frank Gehry Returns to the Streets of His Canadian Childhood
2023-07-15.abstract: The renowned architect was back in Toronto for the start of construction on his second project in the city where he was born and raised.

2023-07-16.title: A National Treasure, Tarnished: Can Britain Fix Its Health Service?
2023-07-16.abstract: As it turns 75, the N.H.S., a proud symbol of Britain’s welfare state, is in the deepest crisis of its history.

2023-07-16.title: Small, Hidden and Deadly: Mines Stymie Ukraine’s Counteroffensive
2023-07-16.abstract: To gain ground, Ukrainian forces have to make their way through a variety and density of Russian land mines they never imagined.

2023-07-16.title: As Iraq Tries to Chill Critics, Its Newest Target Is Social Media
2023-07-16.abstract: New regulations on “degrading content” are part of a broader campaign to silence independent voices.

2023-07-16.title: How a Vast Demographic Shift Will Reshape the World
2023-07-16.abstract: The most powerful countries have benefited from large work forces for decades. What happens when they retire?

2023-07-16.title: Netanyahu Released From Hospital After Undergoing Tests
2023-07-16.abstract: The Israeli prime minister spent Saturday night in a hospital after feeling dizziness, but was said to be in “excellent condition” on Sunday.

2023-07-17.title: Russia’s Embassy in Washington is enmeshed in a different kind of war.
2023-07-17.abstract: WASHINGTON — On a warm June night, Benjamin Wittes was seated at a card table across the street from the Russian Embassy in Washington, kicking off his light show.

2023-07-17.title: Russia pulls out of the Black Sea grain deal.
2023-07-17.abstract: The Kremlin terminated an agreement that had allowed Ukraine to export its grain by sea despite a wartime blockade, a deal seen as essential to keeping global food prices stable.

2023-07-17.title: Riots in France Highlight a Vicious Cycle Between Police and Minorities
2023-07-17.abstract: Calls to overhaul the police go back decades. But violent episodes of police enforcement continue. So do violent outpourings on the street.

2023-07-17.title: Kerry Says U.S. and China Must Set Aside Politics to Tackle Climate Change
2023-07-17.abstract: John Kerry, President Biden’s climate envoy, is in Beijing for three days of talks between the world’s largest emitters of greenhouse gas pollution.

2023-07-18.title: Modi and India’s Diaspora: A Complex Love Affair Making Global Waves
2023-07-18.abstract: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has tried to fuse his image to the economic and political power of Indians abroad. They voice both pride and worry in return.

2023-07-18.title: A Current War Collides With the Past: Remnants of World War II in Ukraine
2023-07-18.abstract: World War II has been an ideological battlefield in today’s war in Ukraine, and it is cropping up on the actual battlefield as well.

2023-07-18.title: Mandela Goes From Hero to Scapegoat as South Africa Struggles
2023-07-18.abstract: Nelson Mandela is revered worldwide and celebrated on July 18, his birthday. But at home, a younger generation is disillusioned with the country, his party and the anti-apartheid leader, too.

2023-07-18.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2023-07-18.abstract: Thawing relations between Israel and the U.S.

2023-07-18.title: Australian State Backs Out of Hosting the 2026 Commonwealth Games
2023-07-18.abstract: The state of Victoria cited budget issues in deciding not to stage the multisports competition, originally known as the British Empire Games, raising questions about the event’s fading prestige.

2023-07-19.title: Kissinger Meets Top Officials in China and Gets a Warm Greeting
2023-07-19.abstract: At 100, Henry Kissinger met with China’s top diplomat as well as its defense minister, who last month rejected an invitation to meet with the U.S. defense secretary.

2023-07-19.title: As Sunak Makes His Case to Britons, the Economy Could Undermine It
2023-07-19.abstract: Britain’s Conservative government faces a morass of problems, some new, others longstanding, that are stymying Prime Minister Rishi Sunak.

2023-07-19.title: For These Ukrainian Soldiers, the Fight Is Personal
2023-07-19.abstract: Ukraine lets soldiers from towns occupied by the Russian military join the battle to reclaim them.

2023-07-19.title: Facing a Future of Drought, Spain Turns to Medieval Solutions and ‘Ancient Wisdom’
2023-07-19.abstract: Acequias, a network of water channels created by the Moors over 1,000 years ago, are being excavated and brought back to life to adapt to the crises of climate change.

2023-07-20.title: Protesters Angry Over Quran Burning Storm Swedish Embassy in Baghdad
2023-07-20.abstract: The demonstrators, responding to a protest in Stockholm last month and ahead of one on Thursday, set fire to parts of the building.

2023-07-20.title: Theme Park’s Selective History Appeals to a New Spanish Nationalism
2023-07-20.abstract: Puy du Fou España has drawn visitors with spectacular shows about Spanish history. But part of its success lies in what goes unsaid.

2023-07-20.title: Europe Pushed Tunisia to Keep Migrants Away. The Result Is Harsh.
2023-07-20.abstract: Tunisia has pushed African migrants to scorching no-man’s lands along its borders with little food and water. Under a deal with the European Union, Tunisia agreed to tighten border controls in exchange for aid.

2023-07-20.title: On the Map, Nothing. On the Ground, a Hidden Maya City.
2023-07-20.abstract: In a biological preserve in Mexico’s Campeche State, a team of archaeologists has documented pyramids, palaces, a ball court and other remains of an ancient city they call Ocomtún.

2023-07-20.title: Ukrainian Children With Cancer Fight Their Own War Within a War
2023-07-20.abstract: The war with Russia compounds the struggles of pediatric cancer patients and their families, but it has also prompted positive changes in treatment in Ukraine.

2023-07-21.title: C.I.A. Chief Says Wagner Mutiny Revived Questions About Putin’s Rule
2023-07-21.abstract: William J. Burns gave the most detailed public account yet by a U.S. official of the damage done to President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia by last month’s uprising by the mercenary group.

2023-07-21.title: U.K. Voters Hand Sunak’s Party Two Defeats and a Win in By-elections
2023-07-21.abstract: The governing Conservatives retained Boris Johnson’s former Parliament seat in northwest London, but lost in two districts, including a sobering defeat in northern England.

2023-07-21.title: Why Do Australian Politicians Love Nicknames?
2023-07-21.abstract: To the average voter, ScoMo might sound more comfortable than “Prime Minister Morrison.”

2023-07-21.title: Mariupol State University has graduated its newest class after fleeing its devastated home city.
2023-07-21.abstract: In the before times, there were caps and gowns and canapés, but Mariupol State University could offer only a pared-down ceremony on Thursday for the class of 2023 on its campus in exile almost 400 miles from its ravaged home city.

2023-07-22.title: In Belarus, the Protests Were Three Years Ago. The Crackdown Is Never-Ending.
2023-07-22.abstract: Aleksandr G. Lukashenko brutally repressed those who opposed his claim of re-election as president. The crackdown on dissent has only deepened since.

2023-07-22.title: A Story of Arab Loss Comes to Life at a Kibbutz in Israel
2023-07-22.abstract: A museum founded by pioneering Zionists has been hosting a sweeping retrospective of Palestinian art.

2023-07-22.title: Venezuela’s Oil Industry Is Broken. Now It’s Breaking the Environment.
2023-07-22.abstract: Gas flares and leaking pipelines from Venezuela’s once-booming oil industry, hobbled by U.S. sanctions and mismanagement, are polluting towns and a major lake.

2023-07-22.title: Japan Says It Can Make Coal Cleaner. Critics Say Its Plan Is ‘Almost Impossible.’
2023-07-22.abstract: Japan says that by blending ammonia with coal in its boilers, it can make coal less damaging to the planet. But the technology faces many hurdles.

2023-07-22.title: Malaysia Halts Music Festival After Kiss Between Bandmates Onstage
2023-07-22.abstract: The episode comes as rights groups have warned of growing intolerance against lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender people in the country, where homosexuality is a crime.

2023-07-23.title: Spain’s Vote on Sunday: Here’s What to Know
2023-07-23.abstract: The national elections could see a far-right party enter the Spanish government for the first time since the 1970s.

2023-07-23.title: With Israel in Turmoil, Netanyahu Is Hospitalized for Pacemaker
2023-07-23.abstract: Doctors said Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was “doing very well” after the unexpected procedure early Sunday, but the news only compounded the chaos over a government plan to limit judicial power.

2023-07-23.title: Deadly Russian Strikes Hit Odesa Cathedral and Apartment Buildings
2023-07-23.abstract: Moscow has renewed its attacks on the port city since pulling out of the Black Sea grain deal last week.

2023-07-23.title: It’s Scorching in Iran, and There’s Less and Less Water to Help
2023-07-23.abstract: Water shortages from longtime mismanagement have left people with shrinking options, as temperatures spike and growing poverty makes air-conditioning unaffordable for many.

2023-07-23.title: Weary Soldiers, Unreliable Munitions: Ukraine’s Many Challenges
2023-07-23.abstract: A month of reporting by New York Times journalists found the fighting mostly stalemated and Ukraine facing an array of obstacles against a determined foe.

2023-07-24.title: Russia Destroys Drones Targeting Moscow, and Blames Ukraine
2023-07-24.abstract: Two buildings were hit but there were no reports of casualties. Video verified by The New York Times shows smoke and damage to several buildings.

2023-07-24.title: Your Monday Briefing
2023-07-24.abstract: An important vote on Israel’s political future.

2023-07-24.title: Passenger Boat in Indonesia Capsizes, Killing 15
2023-07-24.abstract: Most passengers traveling on the overcrowded boat survived Monday’s accident off the island of Sulawesi.

2023-07-24.title: Short-Term Pain for Long-Term Gain? Nigerians Buckle Under Painful Cuts.
2023-07-24.abstract: In his first two months in office, President Bola Tinubu has ripped the Band-Aid off Nigeria’s ailing economy. Time will tell if it can heal, but for now, Nigerians are feeling the pain.

2023-07-24.title: Seeking Full Honors, Some Ukrainian Families Wait to Bury Their Dead
2023-07-24.abstract: Thousands of families have buried soldiers in cemeteries across Ukraine in “Alleys of Heroes.” But some have held off, awaiting a version of Arlington National Cemetery.

2023-07-25.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2023-07-25.abstract: Here’s what you need to know.

2023-07-25.title: How Israel’s Supreme Court Might React to the Challenge to Its Power
2023-07-25.abstract: The court could strike down the law passed on Monday that seeks to curb its power. Or it might narrowly interpret it. Or it might simply put off any decision.

2023-07-25.title: Typhoon Makes Landfall in China as Toll Rises in the Philippines
2023-07-25.abstract: Typhoon Doksuri was bearing winds equivalent to a Category 2 hurricane when it hit China’s coast on Friday. Officials in the Philippines said dozens there had died.

2023-07-25.title: Leaving the Front Line in Ukraine — to Train for the Front Line
2023-07-25.abstract: A grueling obstacle course, a few miles from the fighting with Russia, is a final step in the initiation for Ukrainian marines.

2023-07-25.title: China’s Foreign Minister Is Removed After a Month of Silence
2023-07-25.abstract: Qin Gang, a sometime favorite of Xi Jinping, was replaced by Wang Yi, a seasoned diplomat, leaving many unanswered questions about Qin’s fall.

2023-07-26.title: A Commitment to Green Policy Is Tested by an Election Win
2023-07-26.abstract: British conservatives kept a seat in a recent election by opposing an ultralow emissions zone, and some are now questioning ambitious emissions-reduction targets.

2023-07-26.title: ‘They Shoot Without Stopping’: Where Russia Is on the Attack in Ukraine
2023-07-26.abstract: Along the vast 600-mile front line, Russia is mostly playing defense. In the northeast, it is once again mounting fierce offensive operations.

2023-07-26.title: Fiji’s Leader Declines Invitation to China, Saying He Tripped and Fell
2023-07-26.abstract: The prime minister, wearing a bloodstained shirt, said he struck his head on a door. One analyst called it “very convenient.”

2023-07-26.title: Monsoon’s Fury Sours Romance of Wet Season Travel in India
2023-07-26.abstract: Long considered a life-giving panacea, India’s rainy season has become more violent and unpredictable. It is upsetting many rhythms of life, among them, leisure travel.

2023-07-27.title: Biden and Meloni May Find Common Ground at White House
2023-07-27.abstract: The president has been wary of the Italian prime minister’s hard-right politics. But when they meet on Thursday, they may see eye to eye on key issues.

2023-07-27.title: Life Under U.K. Sanctions: Chauffeurs, Chefs and $1 Million Allowances
2023-07-27.abstract: Despite tough talk, Britain’s new sanctions program against Russia’s invasion of Ukraine has proved shaky. Some oligarchs have received generous exemptions. Officials have at times overreached.

2023-07-27.title: The Korean War in Photos, 70 Years After the Truce
2023-07-27.abstract: American troops led United Nations forces to defend South Korea against the North. The war ended with a cease-fire agreement and a Korean Peninsula that is still divided.

2023-07-27.title: South Korea Unveils Harry Truman Statue
2023-07-27.abstract: The 13.8-foot statue, unveiled in Dabu-dong, Gyeongsangbuk-do, South Korea, honors former President Truman on the 70th anniversary of the Korean War armistice.

2023-07-27.title: The next stage of the counteroffensive appears to be taking shape.
2023-07-27.abstract: Ukrainian forces trying to punch through Russian lines are facing perhaps their biggest test of the war as, according to two Pentagon officials, Kyiv begins the main thrust of its counteroffensive, pouring the bulk of their Western-trained reserves into the fight to sever Moscow’s hold on the south.

2023-07-28.title: Japan Affirms Plans for Deeper Regional Ties to Counter China
2023-07-28.abstract: In a defense white paper, Tokyo presents its case for engagement with allies to respond to threats posed by China and by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

2023-07-28.title: First Responders Arrive at Capsized Passenger Ferry in the Philippines
2023-07-28.abstract: Strong winds from Typhoon Doksuri capsized the vessel, killing dozens of people. The Philippine Coast Guard said they rescued at least 40.

2023-07-28.title: O’Connor Was a Lonely Voice for Change, Until Ireland Changed With Her
2023-07-28.abstract: Sinead O’Connor shocked her strongly Catholic country when she tore up the pope’s picture. But the Church’s sexual abuse scandals made Ireland more secular, and more understanding of her criticisms.

2023-07-28.title: As Heat Waves Intensify, Europe’s Cities Rely on Age-Old Ways to Stay Cool
2023-07-28.abstract: Architectural styles and construction materials can significantly reduce the need for air-conditioning during heat waves, according to sustainable architecture experts.

2023-07-28.title: He Held Up a Bank to Get His Own Money
2023-07-28.abstract: Amid severe limits on withdrawals and a yearslong financial crisis, Lebanese depositors are resorting to force to demand their own money, earning the moniker “the world’s most honorable bank robbers.”

2023-07-29.title: Australia to Fast-Track Missile Production for U.S. Exports
2023-07-29.abstract: The announcement of this plan, along with steps to expand joint military exercises, was shadowed by the news of a helicopter crash during one such exercise.

2023-07-29.title: ‘Evacuated for the Fifth Time’: Worst Wildfires in Canadian History Persist
2023-07-29.abstract: Wildfires in Canada have forced the displacement of thousands of residents. In Northern Quebec, evacuees from the Cree Nation recently returned home after the smoke cleared, only to evacuate yet again after the fires reignited days later.

2023-07-29.title: Canada Is Ravaged by Fire. No One Has Paid More Dearly Than Indigenous People.
2023-07-29.abstract: The country’s record-breaking fire season has led tens of thousands of Indigenous people to flee their homes and ravaged forests they rely on for sustenance.

2023-07-29.title: Coast to Coast, a Corridor of Coups Brings Turmoil in Africa
2023-07-29.abstract: This past week’s military takeover in Niger completed a domino chain of countries ruled by leaders who seized power by force, fueling instability and presenting a conundrum for the United States.

2023-07-29.title: It’s Been a Hellish Summer for the Mediterranean. And It’s Not Over.
2023-07-29.abstract: Record heat and wildfires have turned summer holidays into nightmares and called into question the future of cherished tourist destinations.

2023-07-30.title: A Climate Warning from the Cradle of Civilization
2023-07-30.abstract: How extreme temperatures and dwindling water are pushing the Fertile Crescent toward the brink.

2023-07-30.title: Russia Says 2 Drones Hit Buildings in Moscow in Latest Wave of Attacks
2023-07-30.abstract: The strike was the third in the past week in Moscow, a sign of how no city in Russia or Ukraine appears to be safe from the war. Russia blamed Ukraine, which has yet to comment.

2023-07-30.title: ‘We Are a Romantic Country’: On the Set of a Steamy Hit in Italy
2023-07-30.abstract: Italy falls for “Mare Fuori,” a television melodrama about the inmates of a juvenile detention center who pass the time making out — when not scowling at or occasionally stabbing one another.

2023-07-30.title: Could Ethnic Conflict in India Become an Issue Modi Cannot Ignore?
2023-07-30.abstract: India’s opposition is trying to force the prime minister to address the violence in Manipur, which has killed more than 150 people and displaced 60,000 others.

2023-07-30.title: In Israel, High Stakes for High Court: Democracy’s Fate
2023-07-30.abstract: From Hungary to India to Brazil, how judges responded to attacks on their independence helped decide whether would-be autocrats prevailed in constraining the courts.

2023-07-31.title: Tide of Terror Shifts in Haiti as U.S. Nurse and Her Child Are Abducted
2023-07-31.abstract: This, along with other recent kidnappings, may signal the end of a brief respite, as gangs tighten their grip after being targets of vigilante violence.

2023-07-31.title: Moscow’s attacks are stoking hatred of Russia in Odesa.
2023-07-31.abstract: Standing on a bridge overlooking the road to Odesa’s main port, Nina Sulzhenko surveyed the damage wrought by a recent Russian missile strike: The House of Scientists, one of the Ukrainian city’s best-loved buildings, was in shambles. The mansion’s destroyed gardens spilled down over a ruined residential complex, and burned bricks lay strewn across the sidewalk.

2023-07-31.title: The Country That Bombs Its Own People
2023-07-31.abstract: Visual evidence, data and interviews show that the Myanmar military’s campaign of terror, which began after a coup sparked widespread resistance, is getting worse.

2023-07-31.title: The Brains Behind Netanyahu’s Judicial Overhaul
2023-07-31.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel is the face of his government’s effort to reduce judicial power. His justice minister, Yariv Levin, is the main architect.

2023-07-31.title: A Spending Boom Fuels Russia’s Wartime Economy, Raising Bubble Fears
2023-07-31.abstract: The economic strength has helped to maintain popular support for Vladimir Putin’s war, but some have warned the state-led spending is threatening the country’s financial stability.
